titlestmt.titleproper: Guide to the Barbara Guest Papers
titlestmt.author: By Beinecke staff

publicationstmt.publisher: Beinecke Rare Book and Manuscript Library

maintenanceagency.agencyname: Beinecke Rare Book and Manuscript Library

maintenanceevent.eventdescription: This finding aid was produced using ArchivesSpace on Tuesday October 2, 2018 at 03:01

corpname.part: Beinecke Rare Book and Manuscript Library

accessrestrict.head: Conditions Governing Access
accessrestrict.p: The Barbara Guest Papers are open for research.
Boxes 144-152 (audiovisual material): Restricted fragile material. Consult Access Services for further information.
Boxes 153-154 (born digital material): Restricted fragile material. Access copies of digital files may be requested. Consult Access Services for further information.

acqinfo.head: Immediate Source of Acquisition
acqinfo.p: Purchased from James S. Jaffe on the Eugene G. O'Neill Memorial Fund, the George B. Alvord Fund, the Danford N. Barney, Jr. Fund, the Sinclair Lewis Fund, and the Ezra Pound Archive Fund, 1993.
Purchased from Granary Books on the Ezra Pound Archive Fund, 1997.
Purchased from James J. Jaffee on the Sinclair Lewis Fund, 2002.
Purchased from James S. Jaffe on the Alfred Z. Baker, Jr. Fund, the Sinclair Lewis Fund, the Thornton Wilder Memorial Fund, 2007.
Purchased from James S. Jaffe on the Elizabeth Wakeman Dwight Memorial Fund, 2008.

arrangement.head: Arrangement
arrangement.p: Organized into nine series: I. Correspondence, 1940-2006. II. Writings, 1953-2004. III. Personal Papers, 1943-2006. IV. Professional Papers, X. V. Photographs, circa 1923-2004. VI. Artwork, 1953-1995. VII. Printed Material, 1960-2004. VIII. Audiovisual Material, 1963-2000. IX. Born Digital Material, 1983-2006.

prefercite.head: Preferred Citation
prefercite.p: Barbara Guest Papers. Yale Collection of American Literature, Beinecke Rare Book and Manuscript Library, Yale University.

processinfo.head: Processing Information
processinfo.p: Former call numbers are Uncat MSS 402, Uncat MSS 947, Uncat MSS 1025, Uncat ZA MS 271, and Uncat ZA MS 544.
Unless a folder title contains the title of an artistic work, all quotations represent folder title supplied by Guest.
This finding aid may be updated periodically to account for new acquisitions to the collection and/or revisions in arrangement and description.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: The Barbara Guest Papers span 1923 to 2006 and document Guest's life as a prominent poet, her personal and professional relationships with other writers, and her personal life as a wife, mother, and friend. The papers include drafts of writings, correspondence, personal and professional papers, photographs, artwork, printed material, audiovisual material, born digital material, artwork, and a typewriter.

userestrict.head: Conditions Governing Use
userestrict.p: The Barbara Guest Papers is the physical property of the Beinecke Rare Book and Manuscript Library, Yale University. Literary rights, including copyright, belong to the authors or their legal heirs and assigns. For further information, consult the appropriate curator.

subject.part: LCSH 150 ‡a American literature ‡z United States ‡v Archives

arrangement.head: Arrangement
arrangement.p: Organized into two subseries: General correspondence and Other correspondence

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Correspondence includes correspondence with friends, family, colleagues, and other individuals, relating to Guest's professional and personal life.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: General correspondence subseries contains letters from individuals to Guest. Correspondents notably represented are Johannes Bellharz, Mei-Mei Berssenbrugge, Susan Clark, Robert Dash, Double Day & Company, Anne Dunn, Rachel Blau DuPlessis, Helen Frankenthaler, Kathleen Fraser, and Rena Rosenwasser. Enclosures such as writing drafts and photographs are noted when present.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Includes a photograph of Mary Abbott, 1999

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Includes photocopies of poems by Abish

did.unittitle: Adonah, David at Brown University Library

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Includes photograph of ZKM Museum in Karlsruhe, Germany and corresponding exhibition catalog, 2003; and unidentified photograph of performers, 2006

did.unittitle: Beinecke Rare Book and Manuscript Library
California College of Arts and Crafts

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Includes a poem draft "Practising Dying (III) for Barbara Guest"1980 July 28

did.unittitle: Columbia: A Magazine of Poetry and Prose

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Includes proof of Guest's "Shelley in the Navy-Colored Chair"

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Includes poem draft by Douglas Messerli "Harp on a Mountain of Glass for Barbara Guest"

did.unittitle: Institute of American Indian Arts Press
Jack Kerouac School of Disembodied Poetics
John Jay College of Criminal Justice
Library of the Society of the Four Arts
Los Angeles County Museum of Art

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Includes photographs of Guest's living space interiors

did.unittitle: Pennsylvania Academy of the Fine Arts

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Includes photograph of two unidentified people

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Includes notes about doctor appointments that seem unrelated but were enclosed with correspondence

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Includes page proofs for Barbara Guest's poem "First Prints"

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Other correspondence subseries contains family correspondence kept by Guest, including letters from her children, Hadley and Jonathan; drafts and unsent letters from Guest to various individuals; correspondence related to specific topics and correspondents grouped together by Guest; and postcards kept by Guest. Third party and unidentified correspondence are filed at the end of the subseries. Enclosures are noted when present.

did.unittitle: Clippings on Alexander Calder and Charles H. Higgins
Note, menu for event, Hotel Rembrandt receipt, The Allen-Stevenson School brochure, and typed note from Higgins to Guest

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Includes passports, clippings, academic records, Hadley Guest's cut hair, family photographs, negatives

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Jonathan and Hadley, 1960s; and from John and Louise Pelzel (uncle by marriage and aunt)

did.unittitle: Letters from Barbara Guest to various individuals
Wesleyan University Press regarding Durer in the Window: Reflections on American Art in the 1950s and 1960s by Barbara Guest
Correspondence with Tim Schaffner regarding the Schaffner Press Edition of Herself Defined
Correspondence with Sun and Moon Publisher Douglas Messerli regarding Guest's Fair Realism with drafts for book
Folder of correspondence grouped together by Guest

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Enclosure (letter to Crane from unidentified sender)

arrangement.head: Arrangement
arrangement.p: Organized into two subseries: Writings by Guest and Writings by others

arrangement.head: Arrangement
arrangement.p: Organized into eight groupings: Poetry, Prose, Drama, Essays, Herself Defined: The Poet H. D. and Her World, Notes, Lectures by Guest, and Other writings.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Poetry contains drafts of Guest's poems. Notable poetry publications represented include Bandusia, Blurred Edge , The Countess from Minneapolis , Defensive Rapture , Minatures and Other Poems , Poems , and Quilts . Drafts grouped together by Guest and unsorted poetry drafts and related papers are filed at the end of the subseries.
Prose contains drafts of Guest's prose writings, including her novel, Seeking Air , and her prose poetry work, Rocks on a Platter: Notes on Literature.
Drama contains Guest's dramatic writings, icnluding "The Execution of Mary Stewart", "The Lady's Choice" and "The Office".
Essays contains notebooks of essay drafts kept and titled by Guest, "Notebooks 1-8", and other essays, including essays on H.D. and other artists and writers.
Herself Defined: The Poet H. D. and Her World contains correspondence, research notes, photographs, and working drafts of Guest's notable biography on H.D.
Also in the subseries are various notes kept by Guest, lectures written and presented by Guest upon invitation, reviews by Guest on others' works, and drafts of Guest's bibliography.
Material in this subseries includes handwritten, typescript, and composite drafts. Enclosures are noted when present.

did.unittitle: All Elegies Are Black and White

scopecontent.p: 1 handwritten (on back of another typescript poem); 1 typescript, corrected

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Writings by others contains writing drafts of others that various writers shared with Guest. Most notable among the subseries are writings by Mei-Mei Berssenbrugge and Sara Lundquist. Many of the writings are on Guest and her writing. Guest grouped essays on her written by various individuals together and these folders are filed at the end of the subseries.
Also in the subseries are reviews on works by Guest and interview with Guest.

arrangement.head: Arrangement
arrangement.p: Organized into three subseries: Dayplanners and calendars, Diaries and notebooks, and Other papers

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Personal Papers includes dayplanners, calendars, diaries, and notebooks kept by Guest. They are all dated. Other papers include material relating to Guest's death and funeral and services, such as writings on Guest that were shared during her memorial service, the guest book for her funeral, and various printed obituaries. Also present is material collected by Guest relating to her family's history and genealogy.

did.unittitle: "The Kingdom of the Pool" and undated
Atlases of Los Angeles City and County
Barbara Guest Funeral, Memorial Service, and Tributes
Writings by others on Guest, including Peter Gizzi's piece on Barbara Guest in the Journal of the Academy of American Poets, 2006
Guest book for Barbara Guest's funeral
ITT Hartford account statement and printed material
"Barbara Guest, born Barbara Ann Pinson, 1920 September 6; Family history - Pinsons/Tabors

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Includes photographs of old photographs, letters, obituaries

did.unittitle: "Recent family histry of Barbara Guest: Born Barbara Ann Pinson, 1920 in Wilmington, North Carolina

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Photocopies of correspondence and other family material

processinfo.head: Processing Information
processinfo.p: The material in this series was previously arranged into folders and various groupings by Guest. Specific subjects and meaningful order were not discernable and therefore folder labels were not created. Further processing is required.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: This series includes groupings of material prepared by Guest, including printed material, correspondence, writings drafts, and other papers relating to Guest's professional life.

did.unittitle: Unsorted papers arranged into folders by Guest
Unsorted papers arranged into folders by Guest
Unsorted papers arranged into folders by Guest
Unsorted papers arranged into folders by Guest
Unsorted papers arranged into folders by Guest
Unsorted papers arranged into folders by Guest
Unsorted papers arranged into folders by Guest
Unsorted papers arranged into folders by Guest

arrangement.head: Arrangement
arrangement.p: This series is organized into four subseries: Photographs of Guest and others, Portraits, Photograph albums, and "Photographs A-Z".

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Photographs of Guest contains photographs of Guest, her immediate and extended family, friends, colleagues, and other individuals. Portraits are primarily of Guest but portraits of others are present. Photograph albums are of family and friends, Guest's celebration of her Festschrfit at Bonn University, and a photograph album of Guest's plays being performed. "Photographs A-Z" contains photographs foldered and arranged alphebtically by Guest.

did.unittitle: Barbara Guest, Bob Fabian, and Fay Lousner
Guest on ship, the New Amsterdam, to Europe
Hadley Guest by Karen Hagen Liste
Hadley Haden-Guest, Jonathan Higgins, and acquaintance on ship
Various photographs of interiors and John Edwards presidential campaign
Various photographs of interiors and John Edwards presidential campaign
Guest with composer Gian Carlo Menotti
Bryher and Kenwin in Switzerland; Guest and Bryher
Guest on Olive Hill by Frank Lloyd Wright, taken by Edmund Teske
Photograph of sculpture "Tau" by Tony Smith
New York City with friends, Barnard
Fay Lansner and Barbara Guest collaboration
Guest and Kiki Smith, San Francisco
Family photographs sent by Don Tabor
Barbara Guest, Mary Abbott, Rena Rosenwasser
Photograph and clipping from Good Housekeeping magazine on babysitting with photograph of Hadley
Frost medal awards in New York City

arrangement.head: Arrangement
arrangement.p: Organized into four subseries: Reel-to-reel audiotapes, Audiocassettes, Videocassettes, and Sound discs.

accessrestrict.head: Conditions Governing Access
accessrestrict.p: Boxes 144-152 (audiovisual material): Restricted fragile material. Consult Access Services for further information.

processinfo.head: Processing Information
processinfo.p: All titles are transcribed from labels on the material.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: This series includes reel-to-reel audiotapes, audiocassettes, videocassettes, and phonograph records. The material contains readings, interviews, research material, and other related content.

Guest, Barbara. Collage for Rocks on a Platter bookjacket

arrangement.head: Arrangement
arrangement.p: Organized into two subseries: Publications related to Guest and Printed material collected by Guest

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: This series contains printed material related to Guest and printed material assembled by Guest.

did.unittitle: Publications related to Guest in boxes 157-161
Guest, Barbara. "On the Way to Dumbarton Oaks," from The Hasty Papers.
Guest, Barbara. "The Everly Brothers," from C."
Poems by Guest (review), from The Sunday Star.
The Blue Stairs by Guest (review), from The Virginia Quarterly Review.
Hamtpon, Brigit. "A Dish of a Different Complexion," from Cooking.
Seeking Air by Guest (review), from The Washington Post.
Lally, Michael. Seeking Air by Guest (review), from The American Book Review.
Lally, Michael. Seeking Air by Guest (review), from The American Book Review
The Location of Things by Guest (listing), from Poetry on Tape.
Guest, Barbara. "Radical Poetics and Conservative Poetry," from Scarlet.
[Brief bio of Guest], from Poets House flier.
Guest, Barbara. "Motion Pictures," from The American Poetry Review.
Fraser, Kathleen. "Barbara Guest: The Location of Her," from Poetry Flash.
Einzig, Barbara. "The Surface as Object: Barbara Guest's Selected Poems," from The American Poetry Review.
Guest, Barbara. "Motion Pictures," from The American Poetry Review.
Guest, Barbara. "A Talk on 'Startling Maneuvers'," from Poetry Project.
Guest, Barbara. Several poems, from If.
Guest, Barbara. "Two Poems," from The American Poetry Review.
Guest, Barbara. "An Emphasis Falls on Reality," from Meet.
Kunin, Aaron. "Barbara Guest: You Are Now Leaving Modernity," from Rain Taxi.
The Countess from Minneapolis by Guest (listing), from Burning Deck Books.
Yau, John. "O'Hara and Ashbery: The Poet as Art Critic," from The American Poetry Review.
Kaufman, Erica. The Red Gaze by Guest (review), from The Poetry Project Newsletter.
Stephenson, Shelby. "Burning Deck Offers Barbara Guest, Others," from [unidentified newspaper].
Ackroyd, Peter. [Article about Guest], from Consumer Digest.

accessrestrict.head: Conditions Governing Access
accessrestrict.p: Boxes 153-154 (born digital material): Restricted fragile material. Access copies of digital files may be requested. Consult Access Services for further information.

processinfo.head: Processing Information
processinfo.p: All titles are transcribed from Guest's labels on the material.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: The series contains 3.5 and 5.25 inch floppy disks, CDs and a CD-ROM. The materials store drafts and readings by Guest and other writers.

unittitle.emph: Departure & Its Fountains: Five American Women Poets Read From Their Works

did.unittitle: Barbara Guest reading of her poem "Dissonance Royal Traveler," for KAOS radio, State College Olympia, Washington with Professor Leonard Schwartz program coordinator
Barbara Guest talks given at Mills College, Oakland California
Barbara Guest: Memorial Tribute and Unitarian Church SF State Poetry Center

New York: A Documentary Film: The Center of the World, Episode Eight: 1946-2003, PBS Home Video

did.unittitle: Barbara Guest, bibliography [parts 1 and 2] and poems Hadley Haden Guest stories
David Pinson, brother of Barbara Ann Pinson/Guest, 1983 age 64. Boone North Carolina, Grandfather Mountain Appalchians. Photo Cathy Pinson, wife
